evals.prompt: Create meeting notes from this: Had a call with Dad and Ellen today about the restaurant menu. Dad wants to cut 3 items from dinner - the lamb chops ($42 food cost is too high), the tuna tartare (low seller, 2 orders per week avg), and the mushroom risotto (overlaps with the truffle pasta). Ellen pushed back on cutting the risotto because it's the only vegetarian entree. Decision: keep risotto, cut the other two. Dad wants Chris to develop two replacement dishes by Friday - one seafood, one meat, both under $18 food cost. Ellen volunteered to handle the menu reprint with the designer. Also talked about the bar program - Dad thinks we should add a mezcal section, 4-5 bottles. No decision on that yet, just exploring. Ellen mentioned the wine distributor is raising prices 8% in May, we need to lock in current pricing before then.
evals.expected_output: A well-structured MN file with frontmatter, attendees (Dad, Ellen, Holden), 2-3 topic sections (Menu Cuts, Replacement Dishes/New Menu Items, Bar Program and/or Wine Pricing), with inline Decisions and Actions. No bottom-level consolidated Action Items. Bold on key details like $42, $18, 8%, Friday. Omits empty subsections.

assertions.text: File created at Work Vault/01_Notes/Meetings/ with MN prefix and today's date
Frontmatter includes date created, tags with meeting, and category: Meeting
Attendees section lists exactly Dad, Ellen, and Holden (or Patrick) - no inferred attendees
Decision to keep risotto and cut lamb chops + tuna tartare is captured under a Decisions subsection
Action items have owners: Chris (replacement dishes by Friday), Ellen (menu reprint)
Key dollar amounts ($42, $18, 8%) are bolded
Empty subsections are omitted entirely (no blank Decisions/Plans/Actions headers)
No em dashes used anywhere in the document
No consolidated Action Items section at the bottom of the document
Bar/mezcal discussion captured as Plans (no decision made) not Decisions

evals.prompt:
Create meeting notes from this transcript:

Speaker 1 (10:14 AM): Ok so let's talk about the portal timeline. Where are we on the user-facing pages?
Speaker 2 (10:14 AM): The main dashboard is done, meetings page is done, the work themes page needs about two more days. I'm blocked on the participant attribution because the meeting table has duplicate IDs.
Speaker 1 (10:15 AM): What do you mean duplicate IDs?
Speaker 2 (10:15 AM): The meetings table and portal_meetings table both have records for the same meetings but with different IDs. When I try to join participant data it creates duplicates.
Speaker 1 (10:16 AM): Ok that sounds like it needs to be fixed at the database level. Can you write up what the fix looks like?
Speaker 2 (10:16 AM): Yeah I already have a PIC for it actually. The fix is to unify the tables - merge portal_meetings into meetings with a source column.
Speaker 1 (10:17 AM): Good. How long?
Speaker 2 (10:17 AM): Half a day maybe. But it blocks the participant attribution which blocks work themes.
Speaker 1 (10:17 AM): Ok prioritize that then. Get the table unified first, then attribution, then work themes. What about the KB?
Speaker 2 (10:18 AM): KB is on track. The AI agent scope controls are working. I need to add the expansion tools Patrick asked for - the ability to break down content into sub-steps.
Speaker 1 (10:19 AM): When can we demo to the team?
Speaker 2 (10:19 AM): End of this week if the portal blockers clear.
Speaker 1 (10:19 AM): Let's aim for Friday afternoon then. I'll get everyone on a call.

Attendees were me and Dad.
evals.expected_output: MN file with 2 topics (Portal Timeline, KB Status), proper speaker attribution mapping Speaker 1=Dad and Speaker 2=Holden based on the attendee note. Actions for Holden (unify tables, attribution, work themes sequence) and Dad (schedule Friday demo call). Bold on timeline details.

assertions.text: Speaker 1 mapped to Dad and Speaker 2 mapped to Holden based on context cues
The duplicate ID / table unification blocker is captured with technical detail
Priority sequence is clear: table unification → participant attribution → work themes
KB discussion is a separate topic section, not mixed into portal
Friday demo target is captured as either a Decision or Action with Dad as owner for scheduling
Times from transcript are not included in the meeting note body (they're noise)
No inferred attendees beyond the two explicitly stated

evals.prompt: /create-MN We had a quick 5 min sync with Pablo about the Odoo POS crash that happened yesterday. The system froze during dinner service around 7:30 PM, 3 terminals went down simultaneously. Pablo restarted the server and it came back but we lost about 20 minutes of orders. He thinks it's a memory issue - the server only has 4GB RAM and Odoo recommends 8GB minimum for multi-terminal setups. Decision: upgrade the server RAM to 16GB this week. Pablo will coordinate with the hosting provider. I told him to also set up monitoring alerts so we catch it before it crashes next time.
evals.expected_output: Short, focused MN with 1 topic (Odoo POS Crash / Server Issue), capturing the incident details, root cause analysis, and actions. Should be concise - not over-structured for a 5-minute sync.

assertions.text: Attendees lists Holden and Pablo only
Incident specifics preserved: 7:30 PM, 3 terminals, 20 minutes of lost orders, 4GB vs 8GB RAM
Key numbers are bolded (7:30 PM, 3 terminals, 20 minutes, 4GB, 8GB, 16GB)
Decision captured: upgrade to 16GB RAM this week
Two actions with Pablo as owner: coordinate RAM upgrade, set up monitoring alerts
Note is appropriately brief for a 5-minute sync - not over-padded
No Plans subsection (everything discussed had clear decisions/owners)
